title: grindelia adenodonta
intro: Grindelia adenodonta, also known as Gum Plant or Sticky Weed, is a biennial plant native to North America, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The medicinal properties of Grindelia adenodonta lie in its leaves, stems, and flowers, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The health benefits of the plant include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in soothing sore throats, coughs, and skin irritations. Grindelia adenodonta can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also used in the production of salves and ointments for topical application.
